title: Podman 5.6.0 breaks linux/amd64 image builds and (in some cases) runtime on Apple Silicon Macs
body:
### Issue Description

After upgrading to Podman 5.6.0 we see two separate but related problems:

1) Runtime: Go microservices built by our CI pipelines for linux/amd64 (used in production) no longer run in our local developer environment on Apple Silicon Macs. Our local environment is a KIND cluster running locally; after the Podman upgrade all services panic at startup. It behaves as if Rosetta isn’t active, even though Rosetta is enabled and `podman run ...` works for simple runs.

2) Build: Attempting to build images for linux/amd64 locally also triggers a panic during the build process.





### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the build issue:
Copy the following files to a folder and run `podman build .`

```Dockerfile
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 golang:1.24-alpine

WORKDIR /app

COPY go.mod .

COPY main.go .

ENV GOOS=linux
ENV GOARCH=amd64
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0

RUN go build -o test ./

EXPOSE 8080

CMD ["/app/test"]
```

main.go
```go
package main

import (
        "fmt"
        "net/http"
)

func main() {
        http.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
                fmt.Fprintln(w, "Hello, world!")
        })

        if err := http.ListenAndServe(":8080", nil); err != nil {
                panic(err)
        }
}
```

go.mod
```
module podman-test

go 1.24
```

I let me know if you need a complete example for the KIND cluster too, but it's basically just to create a cluster with `kind create cluster`, and then deploy a (go?) service image built only for linux/amd64.

```txt
> kind version
kind v0.29.0 go1.24.3 darwin/arm64
```

### Describe the results you received

The build process panics

### Describe the results you expected

The image to be built successfully

### podman info output

```yaml
[systeminfo.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21914198/systeminfo.txt)
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Downgrading to podman 5.5.2 fixes both issues

comments.body:
Rosetta is incompatible with newer linux kernels atm, apple only fixed this in their next macos version so we had to disable rosetta for all machines in 5.6 as this was preventing us from shipping newer machine OS versions.
https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/
Oh, bummer... thanks for the info 🙂 I'll keep an eye out for an update.

Would you mind adding this to the release notes? I was diagnosing the same problem - the Podman site only points to the release notes, and those don't mention this at all. I only found out this had happened via this issue.
